points.text: Editas Medicine — CRISPR genome editing systems — In vivo medicines pipeline — Announced public offering of up to $319.4 million in common stock and warrants to fund development of transformative CRISPR-based therapies.

Editas Medicine — EDIT-401 — Hypercholesterolemia — Preclinical data in non-human primates demonstrated robust reductions in LDL-C, Lp(a), and ApoB; company priced public offering of 55,555,556 shares at $2.25 to raise ~$125M gross proceeds.

University of Washington / Seattle Children's Research Institute — CRISPR-engineered B cells — Multiple sclerosis (mouse model) — Gene-edited regulatory B cells demonstrated ability to suppress disease-associated T cells and expand in response to autoimmune tissue damage.
Editas Medicine — CRISPR-based in vivo medicines — Serious diseases — Initiated underwritten public offering of up to $319.4 million to advance CRISPR genome editing systems pipeline.

Editas Medicine — EDIT-401 — Hypercholesterolemia — Preclinical NHP data showed significant LDL-C, Lp(a), and ApoB reduction; warrant exercise triggered by Phase 1 data showing >80% LDL-C reduction in ≥3 patients with 1-month follow-up.

Ragan Pitner (University of Washington) — CRISPR-engineered Bregs — Autoimmune modulation — Presented data at 2026 ASGCT annual meeting showing engineered B cells can dampen overactive immune responses in a mouse model of multiple sclerosis.

Ragan Pitner (University of Washington) — CRISPR-engineered Bregs — Multiple sclerosis — Presented data at 2026 ASGCT annual meeting showing engineered B cells secrete regulatory cytokines (IL-10, IL-35, TGF-beta) to modulate helper T cell activation in meningeal B cell accumulation model.

Editas Medicine — CRISPR genome editing commercialization — In vivo CRISPR medicines pipeline — Raising up to $319.4 million via public stock and warrant offering to accelerate development of transformative gene therapies.

Editas Medicine — Gene editing therapy commercialization — EDIT-401 (CRISPR-based) — Priced $125M public offering (55.5M shares @ $2.25) to fund Phase 1 trials; warrants exercisable upon Phase 1 data meeting >80% LDL-C reduction threshold.

Editas Medicine — Capital raise — CRISPR pipeline funding — Pricing public offering of up to $319.4 million in common stock and warrants; Cantor Fitzgerald and Wells Fargo Securities acting as joint book-running managers.

Editas Medicine — Capital raise — Public offering — Priced 55,555,556 shares at $2.25 plus warrants for total gross proceeds of ~$125M (up to ~$319.4M if all warrants exercised); proceeds to advance EDIT-401.

EDIT-401 — Mechanism: CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing targeting PCSK9 — Phase: Preclinical (NHP) — Population: Non-human primates — Comparator: Baseline — Results: Robust reductions in LDL-C, Lp(a), and ApoB observed.

CRISPR-engineered B cells — Mechanism: Gene editing to express regulatory cytokines (IL-10, IL-35, TGF-beta) and modulate antigen presentation — Phase: Preclinical (mouse model) — Population: Multiple sclerosis model — Comparator: Natural Bregs (noted as heterogeneous and less viable for therapy) — Results: Suppression of disease-associated T cells and expansion upon tissue damage.

CRISPR-engineered Bregs — Mechanism: Gene editing to express regulatory cytokines (IL-10, IL-35, TGF-beta) and modulate antigen presentation — Phase: Preclinical (mouse model) — Population: Murine multiple sclerosis model — Comparator: Natural heterogeneous Bregs (noted as unlikely to succeed therapeutically due to heterogeneity) — Results: Suppression of overactive immune responses and expansion upon tissue damage.
